_meta.purpose: Seed presets for the NurseCalc Titration screen + Nursing Considerations cards.
_meta.sourcing: Concentrations/dose ranges grounded in Davis's & Mosby's drug guides, ASHP Standardize 4 Safety, and ACLS references. Nursing considerations PARAPHRASED from Davis's Drug Guide nursing implications — do not reproduce monograph text verbatim. Standard concentrations are institution-variable presets; the prescriber's order and facility policy override. Pediatric dosing defers to a pediatric reference.

doseModes.mgHr: rate is the dose; no weight conversion

drugs.lasa: do NOT confuse dopamine with DOBUTamine

nursing.before: Correct hypovolemia (fluids before pressors). Baseline BP, HR, ECG, urine output, IV site.
nursing.administration: Central line preferred; if no pump, use 60-gtt microdrip. Dose-dependent effects.
nursing.monitor: Continuous BP and ECG, urine output, peripheral perfusion. Titrate to ordered MAP/BP.
nursing.holdNotify: New tachy-/dysrhythmias, chest pain, BP outside ordered range.
nursing.extravasation: Vesicant (boxed warning: tissue necrosis/sloughing/gangrene). Stop, do not flush, aspirate; antidote phentolamine + warm compress.
nursing.teaching: Report chest pain, palpitations, or pain/burning at the IV site immediately.

drugs.lasa: do NOT confuse DOBUTamine with DOPamine
drugs.rangeNote: primarily inotrope (β1); may lower BP via vasodilation

nursing.before: Correct hypovolemia with volume expanders. Baseline VS, ECG.
nursing.monitor: BP (may drop), HR, ECG for dysrhythmias, cardiac output/response.
nursing.holdNotify: Chest pain, SOB, marked tachycardia, numbness/tingling, IV-site pain.
nursing.extravasation: Vesicant. Stop, leave catheter, aspirate, elevate, warm compress; phentolamine antidote.
nursing.teaching: Report chest pain, shortness of breath, or site pain immediately.

drugs.rangeNote: first-line in septic shock; also dosed 0.01–1 mcg/kg/min

nursing.before: Ensure adequate volume. Baseline BP/HR. Central or large-bore antecubital line; avoid lower-extremity veins.
nursing.administration: Have cardiac monitor and resuscitation cart nearby.
nursing.monitor: BP every few minutes during titration (arterial ideal), HR, rhythm, urine output, perfusion. Assess peripheral site q5–15 min.
nursing.holdNotify: BP outside ordered MAP target, new dysrhythmia, signs of poor perfusion.
nursing.extravasation: Potent vesicant → ischemia/necrosis. Stop, aspirate; phentolamine.

drugs.rangeNote: infusion ≠ arrest push dose; also 0.01–0.08 mcg/kg/min

nursing.administration: Verify infusion vs arrest dose. Titrate to response.
nursing.monitor: Continuous BP, HR, rhythm/ECG (dysrhythmia risk), blood glucose (hyperglycemia).
nursing.teaching: Report palpitations, chest pain, anxiety, or site pain.

nursing.monitor: BP and HR — reflex bradycardia as BP rises; urine output/perfusion.

drugs.rangeNote: fixed dose, NOT titrated; adjunct to norepinephrine

nursing.administration: Fixed dose; adjunct in septic shock.
nursing.monitor: BP, perfusion, urine output; watch for peripheral/mesenteric ischemia.

drugs.rangeNote: stable VT: 150 mg over 10 min, then 1 mg/min ×6 hr, then 0.5 mg/min ×18 hr. Use D5W.

nursing.administration: Use D5W; inline filter; central line preferred for prolonged use.
nursing.monitor: Continuous ECG (QT prolongation, bradycardia), BP (hypotension with rapid infusion), IV site (phlebitis).

drugs.rangeNote: bolus 1–1.5 mg/kg; antiarrhythmic alternative to amiodarone

nursing.monitor: ECG, BP; watch CNS toxicity — confusion, slurred speech, perioral numbness, tremor, seizures.

drugs.rangeNote: start 5 mcg/min, titrate; non-PVC tubing ideal

nursing.before: Baseline BP/HR; confirm SBP adequate. Ask about PDE inhibitors (sildenafil/tadalafil) — combination causes profound hypotension.
nursing.administration: Non-PVC tubing (drug adsorbs to PVC). Ordered in mcg/min.
nursing.monitor: BP frequently/continuously, HR (reflex tachycardia), headache (common), chest-pain relief.
nursing.holdNotify: SBP below ordered floor (commonly <90), worsening hypotension.

drugs.rangeNote: start 5 mg/hr, ↑2.5 mg/hr q5–15 min, max 15 mg/hr (ordered in mg/hr)

nursing.administration: Ordered in mg/hr (not mcg/kg/min). Start 5 mg/hr, max 15 mg/hr.
nursing.monitor: BP and HR continuously; reflex tachycardia; rotate IV site per policy (phlebitis).

drugs.rangeNote: start ~0.3 mcg/kg/min, titrate; do not hold max dose long; cyanide risk

nursing.before: Baseline BP, renal/hepatic function. ICU-level monitoring.
nursing.administration: Protect from light (wrap bag). D5W. Fast, reversible.
nursing.monitor: Continuous arterial BP; cyanide/thiocyanate toxicity — metabolic acidosis, AMS, dyspnea, bradycardia, seizures; thiocyanate level on/after day 3.

nursing.before: Baseline aPTT/anti-Xa, CBC/platelets, weight. Never interchange flush vs infusion concentration — read the label.
nursing.monitor: aPTT or anti-Xa per protocol; platelets (HIT); bleeding (gums, urine, stool, IV sites, neuro changes).
nursing.holdNotify: Active bleeding, platelet drop, critical aPTT.

nursing.before: Baseline glucose, potassium. Dedicated line. Two-nurse check.
nursing.administration: 1 unit/mL standard. Never confuse units with mL. Prime tubing (insulin adsorbs to plastic).
nursing.monitor: Finger-stick glucose q1h (q30min during titration); potassium (hypokalemia). Add dextrose per protocol when glucose <250 mg/dL.
Deep tendon reflexes (loss = early toxicity), respiratory rate, BP, urine output, mag level.
